Counter doctrine and intel snapshots for alliance use. Numbers are model-derived or public-dump-derived — see Method before quoting anything as fact.

Each cell: minimum row-type attackers to fully wipe 10 column-type defenders (1 for Dread/Titan) at ≥90% inside the round cap. Defending needs fewer — defenders keep the field on draws. Hover to cross-hair.

Public-dump snapshot (nexuslegacytools.com), fetched 2026-07-13 14:04 UTC. Dumps lag live play by hours — verify anything time-critical in game. Facts only: no strategy is encoded here.

Top 20 alliances by military score, growth rates over the last ~5.0 days of dump history (window shown per snapshot). Click a column header to sort. Facts only; verify anything time-critical in game.

Non-BTC players sorted by distance to the nearest BTC-member planet (Euclidean, system coordinates; every alliance member's planets count as origins — the Nearest BTC column names the closest member, so any member can read their own raid range). Chips: INACTIVE Nd = zero delta on economy and overall score for N consecutive daily snapshots; VACATION = flagged vacation mode; NO ALLIANCE = active player with no alliance tag. Snapshot 2026-07-13 14:04 UTC.

Caveats: vacation players cannot be attacked (tracked for awareness). PM-reported but unverified: inactive players stop producing ~24h after being flagged — treat loot expectations accordingly. Activity is inferred from daily score snapshots (a player can be active without scoring in a given window).

Every station across all six arms. Owner column shows the holding alliance's tag (or "neutral"). Capture column counts down live from the snapshotted captureEndsAt timestamp. Building levels are packed: Dock · Shield · Turret · Storage. Basics = ore + silicates + hydrogen + alloys. Click a column header to sort. Facts only, snapshot 2026-07-13 14:04 UTC.

Trade-pair rates from hub order streams (fan-site aggregation, 2026-07-13 14:29 UTC). Every rate reads as what 1 unit of the first resource fetches — e.g. hydrogen → silicates: 1.77 sil means one hydrogen trades for 1.77 silicates. Pick a hub for local rates, volumes, and live-order counts; "All hubs" shows galaxy-wide averages.

All 150 sectors from the meta dump. Note: sector-control fields (controller alliance, controlled-since) exist in the dump but are not yet populated by the game — this board lights up automatically when sector conquest goes live. Snapshot 2026-07-13 14:04 UTC.

Market hubs

Six sentinel hubs (safe) and six dead-space pirate hubs (contested), one per arm.

Rolling change log, newest day first. Diffed each publish against the previous snapshot. Cap 200 events; older than 14 days pruned. Facts only. Diff snapshot 2026-07-13 14:04 UTC.

Enter the enemy fleet (from a spy report), tick the hulls you can build, and the advisor searches counter-compositions with the battle engine running in your browser — smallest viable fleet first, with each option's exact resource bill (build-screen numbers, no market conversion) so you can weigh it against your own production lanes. It recommends the margined fleet size, not the knife-edge minimum: sim counts are estimates (see Method), so the default adds +25%. Vanilla tech both sides. Carrier comps model the card-confirmed wing buff (+25% ATK / 15% dodge to 16 fighters per carrier).

Enemy fleet

Your options

Where these numbers come from

Counter matrix: ~950 simulation batteries on the community battle engine (nexuslegacytools). Every stat visible on in-game ship cards matches the engine exactly; shield pools remain the model's reconstruction. Round cap, damage variance, and shield regen use the fan site's calibration fitted against 113 logged real battles (constants: 15 rounds, variance 0, regen 0.4; refreshed each publish). Fit quality is rough (mean error ~0.5) — trust composition choices over exact counts. Pure-type duels at vanilla tech; mixed fleets shift rapid-fire economics substantially. The matrix's Carrier row is bare hulls — the card-confirmed 16-fighter wing buff is a mixed-fleet composition, so it lives in the Fleet Advisor (which does model it), not in a pure-type table. Bomber ×3 vs defense buildings also not shown (the engine sims fleet-vs-fleet only).

Targets / Stations / Sectors / Ticker: joined nightly from the fan site's per-arm universe dumps + player score histories. INACTIVE = consecutive daily snapshots with zero economy and overall score delta (a player can be active without scoring); VACATION comes straight from the dump and such players cannot be attacked. The claim that inactive players stop producing ~24h after being flagged is player-reported and unverified. Station stocks, shields, and capture timers are as-of the snapshot stamp; the Ticker is a day-over-day diff of consecutive snapshots, so same-day events can land a day late. Sector-control fields exist in the dump but are not yet populated by the game.

Intel: joined from nexuslegacytools public JSON dumps (players, alliances, per-arm universe data, 7-day histories). Score/ranking snapshots only — no private account data exists in the dumps. The station/planet feed lags live play. Tracked alliances are added as we gather intel on them.

Fleet advisor: runs the same battle engine in your browser — it binary-searches each doctrine composition for the smallest fleet meeting your confidence target, adds your safety margin, and lists the survivors smallest-first with their exact per-resource build bills (no market conversion). Carrier wings use the card-confirmed buff with dodge approximated as bonus EHP. Same model caveats as the matrix: trust the composition choice more than the exact count; the margin exists because the counts are estimates.

Wanted for calibration: shipyard cost screens for Torpedo Frigate / Carrier / Battleship / Bomber, and ANY battle report with 100+ ships per side. Those two things upgrade every number on this page.
